title: Contact
text: I welcome discussions on potential collaborative research projects, including academic partnerships and research collaborations with public institutions or industry. For an overview of my current research interests and agenda, please see the Research page.

title: Contact
text:
Students and Early-Career Researchers
I am happy to hear from motivated students and postdoctoral researchers whose interests align with the areas above. When reaching out, please include:

A CV (PDF)
A brief statement (1–2 paragraphs) outlining your interests and research goals
(Optional) a writing sample or code repository


Possible opportunities

MSc thesis supervision

PhD positions (subject to availability and funding)

Visiting research stays

Fellowship-based positions (see below)

title: Contact
text:
Fellowship Opportunities
Prospective applicants are encouraged to consider external funding schemes. Examples include:

JSPS Postdoctoral Fellowship (PD)
https://www.jsps.go.jp/english/e-pd/
JSPS Overseas Research Fellowship (海外特別研究員)
https://www.jsps.go.jp/english/e-ab/
Overseas fellowships (non-Japanese applicants to Japan)
I can host applicants at Nagasaki University under relevant fellowship schemes.
(For example: JSPS Invitational / institutional programs depending on year.)
Marie Skłodowska-Curie Postdoctoral Fellowships (MSCA PF)
https://marie-sklodowska-curie-actions.ec.europa.eu/actions/postdoctoral-fellowships

If you are considering applying for a fellowship with me as host or collaborator, please contact me well in advance of the deadline.

title: Writing
text:
General tips

Start with a clear research question and scope
Write early; revise often (small iterations)
Prefer short, structured drafts (bullets → paragraphs)

title: Writing
text:

Paper skeleton (Intro/Methods/Results/Discussion)
Reviewer response template
Figure-first writing workflow

title: Writing

Recommended references

(Add 5–10 core references you actually like)

title: Research
text:
Themes

Real-time outbreak analytics

Estimating key epidemiological parameters (e.g., incubation period, serial interval)
Monitoring epidemic trends

Nowcasting (mpox)
Effective reproduction number using wastewater data (see Miyazawa et al. and van Boven et al.)
We contribute to RespiCast forecasting hub




Epidemic modelling for decision making

Designing targeted control strategies

Optimal vaccine allocation using approximated next-generation matrices (Miura et al. (2021) Plos Comp Bio)
Temporal shift in the dominant transmission route for mpox clade Ib (Miura and Leung et al. (2026) Sci Adv)

Heterogeneity

Individual-level infectiousness
Individual-level susceptibility

Scenario modelling

title: CV
text:
Short Bio
Fumi Miura is a Senior researcher at the National Institute for Public Health and the Environment (RIVM) in the Netherlands and is jointly affiliated with Nagasaki University in Japan. His research focuses on infectious disease modelling, with particular emphasis on transmission heterogeneity, contact networks, and the use of epidemiological and alternative non-epidemiological data to inform targeted intervention strategies.


Full CV

Download CV (PDF)



Positions

2023 - Present: Senior researcher

Center for Infectious Disease Control, The National Institute for Public Health and the Environment (RIVM), The Netherlands (with Prof. Jacco Wallinga)

Unit for infectious disease modelling


2020 - 2023: Postdoctoral Research Fellow (funded by JSPS Postdoctoral Research Fellow SPD/CPD)

Center for Infectious Disease Control, The National Institute for Public Health and the Environment (RIVM), The Netherlands (with Prof. Jacco Wallinga)
Center for Marine Environmental Studies (CMES), Ehime University, Japan (with Prof. Kozo Watanabe)

2017 - 2020: Doctoral Research Fellow (funded by JSPS Doctoral Research Fellow DC1)

University of Tokyo, Graduate School of Engineering (with Prof. Kensuke Fukushi)

2016 - 2017: Visiting researcher

Hokkaido University, Graduate School of Medicine (with Prof. Hiroshi Nishiura)

2016: Internship researcher

World Health Organization (Geneva)
Water, Sanitation, Hygiene and Health, Department of Public Health, Social and Environmental Determinants of Health

2015 - 2016: Adjunct Research Fellow

title: About
text:
I am a Senior Researcher at the Dutch National Institute for Public Health and the Environment (RIVM). I also have an academic appointment at Nagasaki University, Japan.
My work focuses on understanding how transmission processes unfold across heterogeneous populations and how epidemic theory can inform practical control strategies.
My recent research topics include:

Real-time outbreak analytics

Designing targeted control strategies

Epidemic theory for decision making

Dimensional compression and interpretability
